title: Mary Jo Duffy

{{Quote|The fact that these characters were so well-liked that other people would tell more stories about them jazzes the daylights out of me. It was just an amazing, amazing time. I gotta say I'm glad I really made the most of it.|Mary Jo Duffy on her time writing for ''Star Wars''|<ref name="Comic Marvels">{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}}</ref>}}
'''Mary Jo Duffy''' is a comic book writer who worked on many of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] comics. Her first ''[[Star Wars]]'' issue was [[Star Wars (1977) 24|''Silent Drifting'']]. She would later go on to be the regular writer on the title beginning with [[Star Wars (1977) 70|''Star Wars'' (1977) 70]] and continue until the end of the series. She created several recurring characters in the series including [[Dani/Legends|Dani]], [[Rik Duel/Legends|Rik Duel]], [[Kiro]], and brought Shira Brie back as [[Lumiya]].

==Biography==
Joining Marvel in an editorial position in late 1978/early 1979, Duffy's first credits as editor appear on [[C:marvel:The Incredible Hulk Vol 1 231|''The Incredible Hulk'' #231]] and [[C:marvel:The Spectacular Spider-Man Vol 1 26|''The Spectacular Spider-Man'' #26]], both cover dated January 1979. She edited the first seventeen issues of Marvel's [[Wikipedia:Rom (comics)|''ROM'']] series, before handing over the reins to [[Allen Milgrom]].

Her writing work besides ''[[Star Wars]]'' comic included [[Wikipedia:Power Man and Iron Fist|''Power Man and Iron Fist'']], [[Wikipedia:Conan the Barbarian|''Conan the Barbarian'']], and [[Wikipedia:Wolverine (comics)|''Wolverine'']]. Among other things, she created the team and most of the members of the [[Wikipedia:Fallen Angels (comics)|Fallen Angels]].

In the 1990s, she worked for other publishers, including DC Comics, where she wrote the first fourteen issues of [[Wikipedia:Catwoman|''Catwoman'']] (1993). For Rob Liefeld's Extreme Studios imprint of [[Wikipedia:Image Comics|Image Comics]], she wrote all twenty-two issues of the first [[Wikipedia:Glory (comics)|''Glory'']] series, between March 1995 and April 1997.
